mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase exceeding 0.5% signals rising inflation pressures, prompting monetary tightening expectations that depress S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite inflationary signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals robust economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days through improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between GDPC1 and UNRATE fails to produce positive returns in > 40% of cases, or joint signal correlation drops below 0.2 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals supply-side contraction, reducing corporate output capacity and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to correlate with negative 20d returns in > 30% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in payrolls (PAYEMS) above 100k signals strong labor market demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through consumer spending optimism.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances with PAYEMS > 100k, or signal correlation drops below 0.15 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 1.2 million units signals weak demand for construction, depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through reduced corporate investment activity.
failure_condition: HOUST declines fail to correlate with negative 60d returns in > 35% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in residential sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals strong demand for real estate, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through sectoral momentum.
failure_condition: Interaction effect between RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive returns in > 45% of cases, or joint signal correlation drops below 0.18 in rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly FEDFUNDS rate above 4.5% signals a tightening cycle, increasing discount rates and depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances with FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.
